perturbation.original_text: 1.38 "Product" means, as applicable, the (a) Intermediate Drug Product, (b) Bulk Vehicle Gel and (c) Finished Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.38 "Product" means, as applicable, the (a) Intermediate Drug Product and (b) Bulk Vehicle Gel.
perturbation.explanation: The change removes "Finished Product" from the definition of 'Product'. This creates a contradiction because throughout the document, obligations are tied to 'Product' without specifying which type, leading to confusion as to whether obligations related to 'Product' apply to 'Finished Product'. This is contradicting in Section 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.6, 2.7, 2.8, 2.9, 2.10, 2.11, 2.12, 2.13, 2.14, 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 3.6, 3.7, 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 5.1, 5.2, 5.3, 5.6, 6.3, 6.4, 7.1, 7.2, 8.4, 8.5, 8.6, 9.3, 10.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Scope of Agreement. Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, MediWound shall Manufacture (or have Manufactured) Product for clinical and commercial use by Vericel and perform the Additional Services as required for completion of the activities contemplated under this Agreement and the License Agreement in accordance with the applicable Specifications, Regulatory Standards and the Quality Agreement. MediWound shall Manufacture and supply Product in exchange for the Supply Price and shall perform the Additional Services for the Additional Service Fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'Finished Product' from the definition of 'Product'. The contradicted text, Section 2.1, refers to MediWound manufacturing 'Product' without specifying which type. Since 'Finished Product' is no longer explicitly included in the definition of 'Product' after the change, it creates uncertainty whether MediWound is obligated to manufacture 'Finished Product' under Section 2.1, leading to a direct conflict and making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable regarding the 'Finished Product'.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 Materials. MediWound shall purchase at its cost and expense all Materials required for Manufacture by MediWound of the Product for supply to Vericel for the Territory pursuant to this Agreement. Any and all forecasts and purchase orders for such Materials shall be placed at MediWound's sole expense and under its sole responsibility. MediWound shall place such purchase orders on a timely basis in order to avoid any undue delay, interruption or other discontinuance in the Manufacture or delivery of the Product. MediWound shall manage and be responsible for all contracts or other arrangements with MediWound's suppliers of Materials ("Suppliers"). Subject to the terms of this Agreement and the Quality Agreement, as between the Parties, MediWound shall be responsible and have liability for all actions and omissions of, and the failure to comply with the applicable terms of this Agreement, applicable Law or Regulatory Standards by the Suppliers in performance of Manufacturing activities for the supply of Products to Vericel for the Territory on behalf of MediWound hereunder. MediWound shall ensure that all Materials conform to the terms of this Agreement, including the applicable Specifications and to the terms of the Quality Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 Raw Materials. MediWound shall purchase at its cost and expense all Raw Materials required for Manufacture by MediWound of the Product for supply to Vericel for the Territory pursuant to this Agreement. Any and all forecasts and purchase orders for such Raw Materials shall be placed at MediWound's sole expense and under its sole responsibility. MediWound shall place such purchase orders on a timely basis in order to avoid any undue delay, interruption or other discontinuance in the Manufacture or delivery of the Product. MediWound shall manage and be responsible for all contracts or other arrangements with MediWound's suppliers of Raw Materials ("Suppliers"). Subject to the terms of this Agreement and the Quality Agreement, as between the Parties, MediWound shall be responsible and have liability for all actions and omissions of, and the failure to comply with the applicable terms of this Agreement, applicable Law or Regulatory Standards by the Suppliers in performance of Manufacturing activities for the supply of Products to Vericel for the Territory on behalf of MediWound hereunder. MediWound shall ensure that all Raw Materials conform to the terms of this Agreement, including the applicable Specifications and to the terms of the Quality Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “Materials” to “Raw Materials” creates ambiguity. The contract uses “Materials” in its defined sense (1.31), including “Bulk Vehicle Gel” and “Intermediate Drug Product”. By switching to “Raw Materials,” it’s unclear if MediWound is responsible for procuring the components *of* Bulk Vehicle Gel or Intermediate Drug Product, or just the materials in their initial, unprocessed state. This directly contradicts Section 1.31, 2.13.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.31 "Materials" means, with respect to a given Product, all raw materials, Bulk Vehicle Gel (where MediWound is supplying a Product other than Bulk Vehicle Gel), Intermediate Drug Product (where MediWound is supplying a Product other than Intermediate Drug Product), supplies, components, excipients, and intermediates, labels and packaging materials necessary to Manufacture and ship such Product in accordance with the applicable Specifications.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Materials" to "Raw Materials" in Section 2.3 creates a contradiction with Section 1.31. Section 1.31 defines "Materials" to include "Bulk Vehicle Gel" and "Intermediate Drug Product," whereas "Raw Materials" typically implies only the initial, unprocessed components. This change introduces ambiguity regarding MediWound's responsibility for procuring the components of Bulk Vehicle Gel or Intermediate Drug Product, contradicting the original definition of "Materials" and the responsibilities assigned within that definition in Section 1.31.

perturbation.original_text: 1.44 "Regulatory Standards" means all applicable Laws within the Territory applicable to the Manufacturing and shipment of the Product or any aspect thereof and the obligations of MediWound hereunder, including, without limitation, (a) the FD&C Act (or similar requirements of the countries for which the Product will be distributed), (b) cGMPs, and (c) the rules and regulations promulgated under or by a Regulatory Authority or any successor agency or other comparable agency thereto as each may be amended from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.44 "Regulatory Standards" means all applicable Laws within the Territory applicable to the Manufacturing of the Product or any aspect thereof and the obligations of MediWound hereunder, including, without limitation, (a) the FD&C Act (or similar requirements of the countries for which the Product will be distributed), (b) cGMPs, and (c) the rules and regulations promulgated under or by a Regulatory Authority or any successor agency or other comparable agency thereto as each may be amended from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: Removing “shipment” from the definition of 'Regulatory Standards' creates ambiguity and potential contradiction. While MediWound is responsible for adhering to Regulatory Standards related to manufacturing, it's unclear if they are also responsible for standards related to the shipment of the product. This is contradicting in Section 2.9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.9 Delivery. (a) Shipping. MediWound shall only ship Products that have been Manufactured and released in accordance with the Specifications. Unless agreed in advance by Vericel and MediWound in writing, MediWound shall not ship (or permit such Third Party packager to ship) any Products prior to approval and release by MediWound in accordance with the Quality Agreement and Regulatory Standards. Unless otherwise agreed upon by the Parties, Products shall be delivered to Vericel Ex-Works (Incoterms 2010), at MediWound's facility (the "Delivery Site") at which point, the title and risk of loss shall transfer to Vericel which shall transfer the Products from the Delivery Site in accordance with cGMP as applicable. MediWound shall notify (or cause such Third Party packager to notify) Vericel at least [***] prior to any shipment of Products.
perturbation.justification: The original text included 'shipment' within the definition of 'Regulatory Standards.' The changed text removes 'shipment,' narrowing the definition. Section 2.9, 'Delivery,' explicitly states that MediWound must ship products in accordance with 'Regulatory Standards.' By removing 'shipment' from the definition of 'Regulatory Standards,' the changed text creates a contradiction. It is now unclear whether MediWound is responsible for adhering to regulatory standards related to shipment, potentially conflicting with its obligations under Section 2.9.
